zh.lifeSummary: 當Hedwig zur Lippe und von Rheda於大約1243年出生在德國諾爾德海-維斯特法倫可萊斯 居特斯洛時，她的父親Bernhard III. zur Lippe40歲，母親Sophie von Arnsberg32歲。她和Otto III. von Ravensberg至少有4個兒子和5個女兒。她去世於1315年3月5日享年73歲，埋葬在德國諾爾德海-維斯特法倫比勒費爾德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Hedwig zur Lippe und von Rheda于大约1243年出生在德国諾爾德海-維斯特法倫可萊斯 居特斯洛时，她的父亲Bernhard III. zur Lippe40岁，母亲Sophie von Arnsberg32岁。她和Otto III. von Ravensberg至少有4个儿子和5个女儿。她去世于1315年3月5日享年73岁，埋葬在德国諾爾德海-維斯特法倫比勒費爾德。

nameMeanings.name: zur
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Ofer, Yael, Amikam, Amram, Ari, Uri.
Jewish (Israeli): artificial name from Hebrew tsur ‘rock’. In some cases it may also be a translation of some original Ashkenazic surname with similar meaning (e.g. Fels or Stein ).
Polish (Żur): nickname from żur ‘sour soup’ (see Zurek ). Compare Schur .

variants.name: Fels
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Klaus, Kurt, Sieglinde, Wolfgang.
German: topographic name for someone who lived near high ground or by a prominent crag, from Middle High German vels ‘rock, cliff’.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): artificial name from German Fels ‘rock’.

nameMeanings.name: Lippe
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German and Jewish (western Ashkenazic): habitational name from the duchy of Lippe, which became an independent state in the 12th century.
German: variant of   Lipp   3.
German: topographic name for someone living on the banks of the Lippe river in Westphalia.

variants.name: Lipp
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Alois, Frieda, Hans, Wendelin, Franz, Konrad, Otto, Rainer, Ralf.
English: nickname from Middle English lippe ‘lip’ (Old English lippa).
English: from the Middle English personal name Leppe, Lippe (pet forms of one or more Old English names containing Lēof- ‘dear, beloved’ as the first element). Compare Lewin 1.

nameMeanings.name: und
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Klaus, Otto, Wilhelm.

English: from the Middle English personal name Mund(e), in many cases representing Old English Munda, a pet form of names like Ēadmund (Middle English Edmund, common in East Anglia). In northern and eastern England the equivalent Old Norse Mundi (a pet form of names like Agmundr) would also have given rise to Middle English Mund(e).
German: variant of   Mundt  .

variants.name: Monday
meanings: English:

from the Middle English personal name Mundi, Mundy, usually a pet form of male personal names ending in -mund or -mond (see Mund , and compare Edmond , Hammond , and Simmonds ). Alternatively, and especially in northern and eastern England, the name might derive from Old Norse Mundi, a pet form of names like Old Norse Agmundr, Middle English Agemund.
nickname from Middle English Mone(n)day ‘Monday’. It is possibly a name given to one born on that day or to a holder of Mondayland, land held on condition of working for the lord on Mondays.

variants.name: Mundt
meanings: German:
nickname for someone with a prominent or striking mouth or whose mouth was in some other way remarkable, from Middle High German munt ‘mouth’.
nickname for a guardian or trustworthy person, from Middle High German munt ‘guardian, protector’.

variants.name: Munson
meanings: English (mainly Essex): patronymic from the personal name   Mund  , meaning ‘son of Mund’. See also   Munn  .
Americanized form of Norwegian and Danish   Monsen  . Compare   Munsen  .
Altered form of   Monson   1, a surname of Swedish origin.

nameMeanings.name: von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of Welsh   Vaughan  .
Chinese: variant of   Wong  .
Cambodian: written វ៉ុន, unexplained.

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

nameMeanings.name: Rheda
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: variant of Rohde (see Rode ). The surname Rhoda is very rare in Germany.

variants.name: Rohde
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erwin, Kurt, Otto, Bernd, Hans, Heino, Heinz, Ewald, Gerhard, Helmut, Hermann, Klaus.
German and Danish: variant of   Rode   2 and a habitational name from any of various places called Rohde or Rohden in Lower Saxony, Saxony, Westphalia, and Hesse. Compare   Rhode   and   Rhody  .

nameMeanings.name: Hedwig
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: from hadu ‘contention’ + wīg ‘war’.

en.lifeSummary: When Hedwig zur Lippe und von Rheda was born about 1243, in Gütersloh, North Rhine-Westphalia, Germany, her father, Bernhard III. zur Lippe, was 40 and her mother, Sophie von Arnsberg, was 32. She had at least 4 sons and 5 daughters with Otto III. von Ravensberg. She died on 5 March 1315, at the age of 73, and was buried in Bielefeld, North Rhine-Westphalia, Germany.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Hedwig zur Lippe und von Rheda est née environ 1243, à Kreis Gütersloh, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Allemagne, son père, Bernhard III. zur Lippe, avait 40 ans et sa mère, Sophie von Arnsberg, avait 32 ans. Elle a eu au moins 4 fils et 5 filles avec Otto III. von Ravensberg. Elle est décédée le 5 mars 1315, à l’âge de 73 ans, et a été enterrée à Bielefeld, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Allemagne.

de.lifeSummary: Als Hedwig zur Lippe und von Rheda um 1243 in Kreis Gütersloh, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Deutschland geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Bernhard III. zur Lippe 40 und ihre Mutter Sophie von Arnsberg 32. Sie hatte mit Otto III. von Ravensberg mindestens 4 Söhne und 5 Töchter. Sie starb am 5. März 1315 im Alter von 73 Jahren und wurde in Bielefeld, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Deutschland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Hedwig zur Lippe und von Rheda è nata il circa 1243 nel Kreis Gütersloh, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Germania, sua padre, Bernhard III. zur Lippe, aveva 40 anni e sua madre, Sophie von Arnsberg, aveva 32 anni. Lei avuto almeno 4 figli e 5 figlie con Otto III. von Ravensberg. Morì il 5 marzo 1315, all'età di 73 anni, e fu sepolta a Bielefeld, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Germania.

ja.lifeSummary: Hedwig zur Lippe und von Rhedaが約1243年にドイツノードルヘイン-ウエストファレンクレイス グuテルスロで生まれた時，父Bernhard III. zur Lippeは40歳，母Sophie von Arnsbergは32歳であった。彼女はOtto III. von Ravensbergとの間に少なくとも4人の息子と5人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1315年3月5日に73歳で死去し，ドイツノードルヘイン-ウエストファレンビエレフエルドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Hedwig zur Lippe und von Rheda(이)가 대략 1243년 독일 노르드하이-베스트파렌 크레이스 그u터슬로에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Bernhard III. zur Lippe(은)는 40세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Sophie von Arnsberg(은)는 32세였다. 그녀는 Otto III. von Ravensberg(와)과 적어도 네 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1315년 3월 5일 73세의 나이로 사망했으며 독일 노르드하이-베스트파렌 비레펠드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Hedwig zur Lippe und von Rheda nasceu aproximadamente 1243, em Kreis Gütersloh, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Alemanha, seu pai, Bernhard III. zur Lippe, tinha 40 anos e sua mãe, Sophie von Arnsberg, tinha 32 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 4 filhos e 5 filhas com Otto III. von Ravensberg. Ela faleceu em 5 de março de 1315, com 73 anos, e foi sepultada em Bielefeld, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Alemanha.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Hedwig zur Lippe und von Rheda родилась около 1243 г. в местности крис гuтирслох, нордраин-вестфеилен, Германия, её отцу по имени Bernhard III. zur Lippe было 40 лет, а матери, которую звали Sophie von Arnsberg, 32. У неё было по крайней мере 4 сына и 5 дочерей от Otto III. von Ravensberg. Она умерла 5 марта 1315 г. в возрасте 73 лет и была похоронена в местности биелефелд, нордраин-вестфеилен, Германия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Hedwig zur Lippe und von Rheda nació aproximadamente 1243, en Kreis Gütersloh, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Alemania, su papá, Bernhard III. zur Lippe, tenía 40 años y su mamá, Sophie von Arnsberg, tenía 32 años. Tuvo por lo menos 4 hijos y 5 hijas con Otto III. von Ravensberg. Murió el 5 de marzo de 1315, a la edad de 73 años, y fue sepultada en Bielefeld, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Alemania.
